description_long: This is a classic black bicorne hat, historically associated with military and naval officers, particularly from the 18th and early 19th centuries. The hat features a broad, crescent-shaped brim that is sharply upturned on two sides, creating its distinctive two-cornered silhouette. A decorative cockade, consisting of a pleated rosette and a ribbon, is attached to one side, adding a touch of formality and regality. The surface of the hat appears to be made from felt or wool, with subtle wear and a matte finish, and the edges are trimmed with a lighter-colored piping. The crown is rounded and rises smoothly from the brim, providing a structured yet elegant profile. This hat would be a striking accessory in an urban parade, historical reenactment, or themed event.
description: A black bicorne hat with upturned sides, a rounded crown, and a decorative cockade on one side. The hat has a matte, felt-like finish and light piping along the edges.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the upturned brim and the decorative cockade on the left side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cockade and the sharply upturned brim edges converging at the front and back.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the smooth, continuous curve of the brim and the absence of decorative elements.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the upturned brim and the back convergence, with no visible cockade.
attribute_brim_style: wide, sharply upturned on two sides
